text: The surgeon skeletonizes the bowel by carefully dissecting along the mesentery of the small bowel, moving proximally towards the next pedicle artery to achieve optimal mobilization and control for anastomosis.

The surgeon navigates underneath the small bowel without difficulty, but encounters challenges beneath the colon due to mesenteric attachment. The mesentery is being skeletonized from the colon to prevent incorporation into the staple line, ensuring proper mobilization. The mesentery visibly falls away from the colon, with the surgeon maintaining a perpendicular orientation to the bowel.
The surgeon demonstrates the skeletonization of the bowel on both sides, facilitating the easy placement of the stapler across the bowel to fire the common staple line and complete the anastomosis.
